info.title: Rapidata Flow API
info.description: The API for the Rapidata Flow service

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow to delete.

get.summary: Retrieves a flow by its ID.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow to retrieve.

get.summary: Returns the distribution of completion durations (in seconds) per completed flow item.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Optional start of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Optional end of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: bucketCount
parameters.description: Number of histogram buckets (default 10, max 100).

get.summary: Returns flow item creation counts bucketed over time.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Optional start of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Optional end of the date range filter (exclusive).

parameters.name: datapoints
parameters.description: Number of time buckets (default 100, max 1000).

get.summary: Returns the distribution of total votes per completed flow item.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Optional start of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Optional end of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: bucketCount
parameters.description: Number of histogram buckets (default 10, max 100).

get.summary: Returns average response counts bucketed over time for completed flow items.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow.

parameters.name: from
parameters.description: Optional start of the date range filter (inclusive).

parameters.name: to
parameters.description: Optional end of the date range filter (exclusive).

parameters.name: datapoints
parameters.description: Number of time buckets (default 100, max 1000).

get.summary: Queries flows with filtering and pagination.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Filter by name.

parameters.name: created_at
parameters.description: Filter by created_at.

post.summary: Stops the specified flow item and triggers partial result processing.

parameters.name: flowItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow item to stop.

patch.summary: Updates the configuration of a ranking flow.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the ranking flow to update.

post.summary: Creates a new flow item for the specified flow.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow to create the item in.

get.summary: Queries flow items for a flow.

parameters.name: flowId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow to query items for.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: The 1-based page index.

parameters.name: page_size
parameters.description: The number of items per page.

parameters.name: sort
parameters.description: Sort fields. Prefix with - for descending order (e.g. -created_at).

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Filter by state.

parameters.name: created_at
parameters.description: Filter by created_at.

get.summary: Retrieves a flow item by its ID.

parameters.name: flowItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow item to retrieve.

get.summary: Returns ranking results with Elo scores for a completed flow item.
get.description: Returns 409 Conflict if the flow item is not yet completed or has no associated workflow.

parameters.name: flowItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow item to get results for.

get.summary: Retrieves the pairwise vote matrix for a completed flow item.
get.description: Returns 409 Conflict if the flow item is not completed or has no associated workflow.

parameters.name: flowItemId
parameters.description: The ID of the flow item to get the vote matrix for.

name.description: The name of the ranking flow.

criteria.description: The ranking criteria used to compare items.

audienceId.description: Optional audience ID. When provided, the flow will only be served to users in this audience.

validationSetId.description: Optional ID of the validation set to use.

startingElo.description: Initial Elo rating for new items. Defaults to 1200.

maxResponses.description: Maximum number of responses per comparison.

serveResponses.description: Number of accepted responses per rapid at which to stop serving. When set, must be less than or equal to MaxResponses. Null defaults to MaxResponses.

serveToResponseRatio.description: Ratio of concurrent serves to max responses. When set, limits serving to avoid over-collection.

serveTimeoutSeconds.description: Time in seconds a user has to submit an answer after loading the task. When set, overrides the global default.

minResponses.description: Minimum number of responses per comparison. Defaults to 1.

featureFlags.description: Optional feature flags to enable for this flow.

targetResponseCount.description: Target average response count per completed item. Enables PID control when set.

pidMinSessionsPerMinute.description: Minimum sessions per minute the PID can set. Defaults to 10.

pidMaxSessionsPerMinute.description: Maximum sessions per minute the PID can set. Defaults to 10000.

drainDurationSeconds.description: Duration in seconds for draining flow items. Defaults to 40.

globalBoostLevel.description: Global boost level for the flow campaign. Defaults to 1.

flowId.description: The ID of the created ranking flow.

datasetId.description: The ID of the dataset to use for this flow item.

context.description: Optional context to provide additional ranking guidance.

timeToLiveInSeconds.description: Optional time-to-live in seconds before the flow item expires.

drainDurationInSeconds.description: Optional drain duration in seconds. When set, rapids are paused this many seconds before TTL expiry to allow in-flight responses to complete.

flowItemId.description: The ID of the created flow item.

FlowItemState.description: The current state of the flow item.

FlowType.description: The type of the flow. Use the _t discriminator instead.

id.description: The unique identifier of the flow item.

flowId.description: The ID of the flow this item belongs to.

datasetId.description: The ID of the dataset used by this flow item.

workflowId.description: The ID of the workflow created for this flow item.

context.description: Optional context associated with this flow item.

failureMessage.description: The failure message if the flow item failed.

expiresAt.description: The expiration timestamp of the flow item.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item was created.

startedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item started processing.

completedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item completed.

failedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item failed.

datapoints.description: The ranked datapoints with their Elo scores.

totalVotes.description: The total number of votes across all datapoints.

index.description: Row labels of the vote matrix.

columns.description: Column labels of the vote matrix.

data.description: The vote counts as a two-dimensional matrix where Data[i][j] is the number of times row i was preferred over column j.

id.description: The unique identifier of the flow.

campaignId.description: The ID of the campaign associated with this flow.

flowItemCount.description: The total number of items that have been added to this flow. Incremented atomically on each item creation.

targetResponseCount.description: Target average response count per completed item. Enables PID-based automatic rate control when set. Null disables PID.

pidProportionalGain.description: PID proportional gain. Controls how strongly the rate reacts to the current error between target and actual response counts.

pidIntegralGain.description: PID integral gain. Eliminates steady-state error by accumulating error over time. Includes anti-windup clamping.

pidDerivativeGain.description: PID derivative gain. Provides damping by reacting to the rate of change of the error, helping prevent overshoot.

pidMinSessionsPerMinute.description: Minimum sessions per minute the PID controller can set. Prevents the rate from dropping to zero.

pidMaxSessionsPerMinute.description: Maximum sessions per minute the PID controller can set. Prevents the rate from exceeding infrastructure capacity.

serveTimeoutSeconds.description: Maximum time in seconds a user has to submit an answer after loading the task. Null uses the system-wide default.

drainDurationSeconds.description: Grace period in seconds after an item stops receiving new serves, allowing in-flight responses to complete.

serveToResponseRatio.description: Ratio of serves to responses used to calculate how many tasks to serve per expected response.

globalBoostLevel.description: Priority multiplier for the flow's campaign. Higher values increase annotator compensation to attract more responses.

audienceBoosts.description: Audience-specific boost levels that override the global boost for targeted demographics.

audienceId.description: Optional audience ID. When provided, the flow will only serve items to users in this audience.

criteria.description: The comparison instruction shown to annotators during ranking tasks (e.g. "Which image is sharper?").

startingElo.description: The initial Elo rating assigned to new items entering the ranking. Standard default is 1200.

kFactor.description: Elo K-factor controlling rating volatility. Higher values cause larger rating changes per comparison. Used as a direct multiplier on the Elo change formula.

scalingFactor.description: Elo scaling factor that determines how rating differences translate to expected win probabilities. A difference equal to the scaling factor gives the higher-rated item approximately 90% expected win rate.

minResponses.description: Minimum number of responses a ranking item must receive before it is considered sufficiently evaluated.

maxResponses.description: Maximum number of responses a ranking item can receive before being removed from active ranking. Null allows unlimited responses.

serveResponses.description: Number of accepted responses per rapid at which to stop serving. Null defaults to MaxResponses.

featureFlags.description: Key-value feature flags that enable specific behaviors for this flow (e.g. rapid_hints).

ownerId.description: The ID of the customer who owns the flow.

ownerMail.description: The email of the customer who owns the flow.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the flow was created.

PidBatchMode.description: How the PID output maps to the campaign rate. Total: used directly. PerBatch: multiplied by active item count. PerBatchTimeWeighted: multiplied by time-weighted active item count based on remaining TTL.

id.description: The unique identifier of the flow item.

datasetId.description: The ID of the dataset used by this flow item.

workflowId.description: The ID of the workflow created for this flow item.

context.description: Optional context associated with this flow item.

failureMessage.description: The failure message if the flow item failed.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item was created.

startedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item started processing.

completedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item completed.

failedAt.description: The timestamp when the flow item failed.

id.description: The unique identifier of the flow.

ownerId.description: The ID of the customer who owns the flow.

ownerMail.description: The email of the customer who owns the flow.

createdAt.description: The timestamp when the flow was created.

level.description: The boost level for this audience.

audienceId.description: Optional audience ID. When provided, the flow will only serve items to users in this audience. Set to null to remove the audience restriction.

criteria.description: The ranking criteria used to compare items.

startingElo.description: Initial Elo rating for new items.

minResponses.description: Minimum number of responses per comparison.

maxResponses.description: Maximum number of responses per comparison.

serveResponses.description: Number of accepted responses per rapid at which to stop serving. Set to null to remove. Must be less than or equal to MaxResponses when both are set.

serveToResponseRatio.description: Ratio of concurrent serves to max responses. Set to null to remove the limit.

serveTimeoutSeconds.description: Time in seconds a user has to submit an answer after loading the task. Set to null to use the global default.

featureFlags.description: Feature flags to enable for this flow.

targetResponseCount.description: Target average response count per completed item. Set to null to disable PID control.

pidMinSessionsPerMinute.description: Minimum sessions per minute the PID can set.

pidMaxSessionsPerMinute.description: Maximum sessions per minute the PID can set.

pidBatchMode.description: How PID output maps to campaign rate. Total: direct rate. PerBatch: multiplied by active batch count. PerBatchTimeWeighted: multiplied by time-weighted batch count.

globalBoostLevel.description: Global boost level for the flow campaign.

Bearer.description: JWT Authorization header using the Bearer scheme

OAuth2.description: OAuth2 authorization header using the Bearer scheme
